steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states what Herodotus wrote about in his Histories: detailed descriptions of chambers, courts, and passages that he called "an unending marvel." The correct choice must reflect only what the passage actually says.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — account of the complex's magnificence: Matches the passage directly — Herodotus described chambers, courts, and passages and characterized them as marvelous. ✓
  • Choice B — construction time: The passage does not mention how long the complex took to build. ✗
  • Choice C — building and prosperity: No causal link between the complex's construction and prosperity appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — how Herodotus arranged his visit: The passage describes what Herodotus wrote, not the logistics of any visit he made. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
